content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Central File                                Date : February 3, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Gina K.

Subject: Michael Eddy Correspondence Dated 7/1/77

After discussing Mr. Eddy's question with Ken Finney, Chief
of the Legal Division, I informed Mr. Eddy that the $60
which was found on the floor next to the buffet table at a
fund-raising dinner would be considered an anonymous contri-
bution and should be turn over to the state.

MICHAEL B. EDDY
ATTORNEY AT LAW
3067 FIFTH AVENUE
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92103
(714) 299-4336

September 26, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: DAWN WISER

Re: Committee to Retain Iris
Sankey, I.D. #761029

Dear Ms. Wiser:

     Please find enclosed a photocopy of my letter to
you dated July 1, 1977. Approximately 10 days thereafter,
you and I had a telephone conversation regarding the decision
of the Fair Political Practices Commission that the $60.00
in cash found on the floor should be placed in the form of a
money order or cashier's check and forwarded to the State of
California.

     If you will recall, I requested that you forward a
letter to me outlining the instructions to forward the $60.00
as indicated in our telephone conversation. To date, I have
not received a letter from you and for the purposes of the
records of the Committee to Retain Iris Sankey, I would appreci-
ate your forwarding a letter outlining same as soon as possible.

     If you have any questions regarding this problem,
please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

MICHAEL B. EDDY

MBE:kas
Enclosure

1568 SIXTH AVENUE
SAN DIEGO
CALIFORNIA 92101
(714) 239-0461

JOHN T. POPKO
BRUCE L. CORNBLUM
JAMES MILLER
MICHAEL B. EDDY
LEE M. WOODLAND
DOUGLAS A. STOODT

PLEASE REPLY TO
SAN DIEGO OFFICE ☒

LAW OFFICES

POPKO, CORNBLUM, MILLER & EDDY
AND

SCRIM AND WALTERS
ASSOCIATED PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

July 1, 1977

12759 POWAY ROAD
OAK KNOLL SQUARE
P.O. BOX 666
POWAY
CALIFORNIA 92064
(714) 748-3133
(714) 292-9965

ROBERT R. SCRIM
R. MICHAEL WALTERS

PLEASE REPLY TO
POWAY OFFICE ☐

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Dawn Wiser

Re: Committee to Retain Iris Sankey, I.D. #761029

Dear Ms. Wiser:

     Please be advised that on June 25, 1977, at the Vacation
Village there was a fund raiser for Iris Sankey. This function
was a buffet dinner and dancing. While Lou Jones (home phone: 747-0388) was going through the buffet he found $60.00 in cash on the floor near the serving line. When this was reported to me I took possession of the $60.00 and made an announcement at the dinner that a sum of money had been found and that if anyone could come forward and identify the denominations of the bills and the sum of money missing then I would return it to the owner. At this point, no one has claimed to have lost the $60.00 that was found on June 25, 1977.

     I realize that the law forbids acceptance of cash contributions of $50.00 or more. At this point there is a report due by this committee on July 31, 1977 and as treasurer I do not know how the $60.00 should be reported. Based on the facts reported to me by Lou Jones, I believe there is no question that the money was dropped by one of the people at the dinner and though we have made every effort to return it no one has yet claimed it.

     The purpose of this letter is to request from you your advice as to how the $60.00 should be reported. Since the report is due on July 31, 1977, I would appreciate your answering this inquiry on or before July 15, 1977. If you have any questions regarding this situation please do not hesitate to contact me.

     Sincerely,

     POPKO, CORNBLUM, MILLER & EDDY

     [signature]
     MICHAEL B. EDDY

MBE/jmm

sections.question: How should the $60.00 in cash found on the floor at a fund-raising dinner be reported, given that the law forbids acceptance of cash contributions of $50.00 or more and no one has claimed the money despite efforts to return it?
sections.conclusion: The $60 which was found on the floor next to the buffet table at a fund-raising dinner would be considered an anonymous contribution and should be turned over to the state.

sections.question_synthetic: Michael Eddy, as treasurer of Committee to Retain Iris Sankey, seeks guidance on how to report and handle $60.00 in cash found on the floor at a fundraising dinner when no one claims it.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the $60 cash found at the fundraising dinner constitutes an anonymous contribution and must be turned over to the state, to be submitted as a money order or cashier's check.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a correspondence chain including: (1) initial inquiry from Eddy dated 7/1/77, (2) FPPC advice memo dated 2/3/78, and (3) follow-up from Eddy dated 9/26/77 requesting written confirmation. The FPPC response is brief and contained in the memo. OCR quality is generally good with minor errors ('annoymous' for 'anonymous', 'turn£;pver' for 'turn over'). Document ID references 78ADV-78-229, suggesting this was advice letter #229 from 1978.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should the $60.00 in cash found on the floor at a fund-raising dinner be reported, given that the law forbids acceptance of cash contributions of $50.00 or more and no one has claimed the money despite efforts to return it?

CONCLUSION: The $60 which was found on the floor next to the buffet table at a fund-raising dinner would be considered an anonymous contribution and should be turned over to the state.

embedding.summary: Attorney Michael Eddy inquired how to report $60 in unclaimed cash found at a fundraising dinner for Committee to Retain Iris Sankey. The FPPC responded that it should be treated as an anonymous contribution and turned over to the state.
